review_summary: The transcript is sufficient for application-oriented questions on the Sports-1M comparison and why C3D is more computationally expensive than 2D video baselines.

question_bank.question: What is the most practical lesson from the Sports-1M comparison of video classifiers?
choices: A simple single-frame baseline can already be strong, so it should be compared before adopting a more complex model
Early fusion always dominates every other approach
Temporal modeling is unnecessary for all video tasks
question_bank.explanation: The reported results show that a simple approach can already perform competitively. That makes it valuable as a baseline before paying the extra complexity cost of more advanced models.

question_bank.question: Why is C3D much more computationally expensive than a 2D VGG-style image model?
choices: It replaces 2D operations with 3D convolutions and pooling over time as well as space
It removes all weight sharing across layers
It trains a separate network for every video frame
It uses only fully connected layers
question_bank.explanation: Moving from 2D to 3D means each operation also processes temporal extent, not just height and width. That extra dimension significantly increases floating-point computation.
